behavior_summary: Provides behavior for a persistent 'fire field' item: a short U8P_FIREFELD::look/description, periodic animation/tick processing that can damage/affect overlapping NPCs, and a hit handler that forwards or reflects incoming hits into actors inside the field.
U8P_FIREFELD::look responds to examination by displaying the label "fire field ".

U8P_FIREFELD::anim runs on animation/tick events: advances or resets animation frames, may spawn a visual/particle helper process (PENT::1179) if none exist for certain frames, scans the field area for overlapping NPCs, plays SFX for special types, and forwards damage (Item::receiveHit) to overlapped NPCs when appropriate.

U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit handles incoming hits: it checks overlaps, filters NPCs, treats items of type 0x60 as a special case (plays SFX and suppresses forwarding) and otherwise computes direction and forwards the hit (damage/type) to overlapping NPCs via Item::receiveHit.
notable_control_flow: Event mapping: U8P_FIREFELD::look = Event 0 (examine); U8P_FIREFELD::anim = Event 2 (tick/animation); U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit = Event 6 (incoming hit).
On specific animation frames U8P_FIREFELD::anim checks Kernel::getNumProcesses(..., PENT::1179) and spawns PENT::1179 when none exists (visual/particle helper).
U8P_FIREFELD::anim creates a temporary list of overlapped NPC ids, iterates it to handle special-type items (type 0x60) and to conditionally apply hits; the list is freed before return.

Both gotHit and anim compute directions (via Item::getDirToCoords / Item::getDirToItem) and call Item::receiveHit to forward damage; they also play SFX and call FREE::1A96 as side effects in particular branches.
open_questions: What concrete gameplay meaning does item type 0x60 represent (it is special-cased but its semantic meaning is inferred)?
What precisely does FREE::1A96 do (visual effect, timing helper, or state change)?
Exact numeric frame values and SFX ids used for logic branches are inferred from comparisons but not named; what are the intended visual/audio assets?
What is the intended behavior of the spawned process PENT::1179 (particle type, lifetime, and interaction with hosts)?

main_function.name: U8P_FIREFELD::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's 'U8P_FIREFELD::look' / examine event for the fire field and present a short descriptive bark ('fire field ').
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when an actor (usually the Avatar) inspects or examines the fire field item.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_FIREFELD::look handler: emits description via Item::bark

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::gotHit
function_map.role: incoming hit handler: inspects overlaps and forwards or suppresses hits to overlapped NPCs; plays SFX for special types

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::anim
function_map.role: animation/tick handler: advances frames, spawns helper process when needed, enumerates overlapped NPCs, applies periodic damage/effects, plays SFX and invokes FREE::1A96

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: engine/item interface to show a short message from the item (used by U8P_FIREFELD::look and sometimes by other flows)

function_map.name: Item::overlaps
function_map.role: test whether a map location/actor id overlaps this item (used to find actors inside the field)

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: get a target's type to special-case type 0x60

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: apply/forward a hit to a target id (used to damage overlapped NPCs)

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: query count of helper processes (used to decide spawning PENT::1179)

function_map.name: spawn (PENT::1179)
function_map.role: spawn a helper process (visual/particle) associated with the fire field

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: auxiliary routine invoked alongside SFX/hit forwarding; semantics inferred (likely visual/timing helper)

function_map.name: U8P_FIREFELD::playSFX
function_map.role: play sound effects for special interactions and frame events

function_map.name: Item::getDirToCoords
function_map.role: compute direction from the fire field to given coordinates (used when forwarding hits)

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: compute direction from the fire field to another item id

external_calls.purpose: Display the short text description 'fire field ' to the inspecting process
Enumerate/check which actors/items overlap the field when a hit is received
Filter overlapped ids to NPCs before applying forwarded hits
Detect special type 0x60 to suppress forwarding and play special SFX
Play a special sound when a special type (0x60) is encountered during hit handling
Obtain the fire field Y coordinate to compute direction toward the hit/target
Obtain the fire field X coordinate to compute direction toward the hit/target
Compute direction byte used when forwarding a hit via Item::receiveHit
Forward the incoming hit (direction, damage/type, source id) to overlapping NPCs
Invoke an auxiliary visual/timing helper alongside certain hit-forwarding branches
Read current animation frame to drive per-frame behavior
Check whether helper PENT::1179 processes already exist before spawning
Create a visual/particle helper process on specified frames if none exists
Advance or reset the fire field's animation frame index
Scan the field area to collect overlapped ids into a temporary list
Filter collected overlaps to NPCs during the scanning pass
Detect special type 0x60 among overlapped NPCs to trigger alternate SFX/behavior
Play SFX either per-item for special types or once after scanning if no special type found
Invoke an auxiliary helper before forwarding damage in the periodic tick
Compute direction from the field to each overlapped NPC before calling receiveHit
Apply periodic damage/effects to overlapped NPCs collected during the animation tick

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides behavior for a persistent 'fire field' item: a short U8P_FIREFELD::look/description, periodic animation/tick processing that can damage/affect overlapping NPCs, and a hit handler that forwards or reflects incoming hits into actors inside the field.
